name: warfarin
mechanism: Inhibits VKORC1, reducing active vitamin K for clotting factors II, VII, IX, X
monitoring: INR weekly until stable, then monthly|Signs of bleeding
food_effect: Vitamin K (leafy greens) antagonizes effect — maintain consistent intake. Grapefruit irrelevant.
notes: CYP2C9 *2/*3 polymorphisms reduce metabolism → need lower dose

name: heparin
mechanism: Activates antithrombin III, inhibiting thrombin and Xa
food_effect: None
notes: HIT risk — monitor platelets. Protamine reversal available.

name: rivaroxaban
mechanism: Direct inhibition of factor Xa, no antithrombin required
food_effect: 15mg/20mg doses must be taken WITH food (fat increases absorption 39%). 10mg can be taken without food.
notes: Avoid if CrCl <15. P-gp and CYP3A4 inhibitors increase exposure.

name: apixaban
mechanism: Direct inhibition of factor Xa
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: Andexanet alfa reversal available.

name: amlodipine
mechanism: Blocks L-type calcium channels in vascular smooth muscle → vasodilation
food_effect: Grapefruit juice inhibits intestinal CYP3A4 → Cmax +35%, AUC +40%. Avoid entirely.
notes: Long half-life → once daily dosing. Minimal negative inotropy vs non-DHP CCBs.

name: lisinopril
mechanism: Inhibits angiotensin-converting enzyme → reduces angiotensin II
monitoring: BP|Renal function (Cr, K+) at 1-2 weeks after start|Cough
food_effect: Potassium-rich foods (bananas, oranges) may increase hyperkalemia risk
notes: Reduce dose in CrCl <30. Contraindicated in pregnancy (teratogenic). ACE cough in 10-15%.

name: enalapril
mechanism: Prodrug → enalaprilat inhibits ACE
food_effect: Food delays Tmax but does not affect AUC
notes: Requires renal activation. Monitor K+ especially with K-sparing diuretics.

name: losartan
mechanism: Blocks AT1 angiotensin II receptor
food_effect: AUC reduced ~10% with high-fat meal — clinically insignificant
notes: Active metabolite EXP3174 is more potent. Less cough than ACEi.

name: metoprolol
mechanism: Selectively blocks beta-1 adrenergic receptors
food_effect: Food increases AUC ~40% for immediate release — take with food for consistency
notes: CYP2D6 PMs have 4-5x higher concentrations. Taper on discontinuation.

name: atenolol
mechanism: Selective beta-1 adrenergic blockade
food_effect: Food reduces Cmax 20% but AUC unchanged
notes: Hydrophilic — renal excretion, reduce dose in renal impairment. Less CNS effects than propranolol.

name: carvedilol
mechanism: Non-selective beta + alpha-1 blockade → combined vasodilation + HR reduction
food_effect: Take WITH food to slow absorption and reduce orthostatic hypotension risk
notes: First-line for heart failure. Significant first-pass effect. CYP2D6 PMs have higher exposure.

name: digoxin
mechanism: Inhibits Na+/K+-ATPase → increased intracellular Ca++ → positive inotropy
monitoring: Digoxin level (6-8hr post-dose)|K+, Mg++, Ca++|ECG|Renal function
food_effect: High-fiber meal reduces absorption 25%. Take consistently relative to meals.
notes: P-gp substrate — many interactions. Hypokalemia potentiates toxicity. Signs: nausea, yellow-green halos.

name: amiodarone
mechanism: Blocks K+ channels → prolongs action potential. Also blocks Na+, Ca++, beta
monitoring: TFTs q6mo|LFTs q6mo|CXR annually|PFTs if symptomatic|Ophthalmology|Thyroid
food_effect: Grapefruit increases levels. Take consistently.
notes: 40-55 day half-life. Inhibits CYP2C9 → warfarin 35-65% dose reduction. Iodine-rich — thyroid toxicity.

name: spironolactone
mechanism: Competitive aldosterone antagonist → Na+ excretion, K+ retention
food_effect: Take WITH food — improves absorption and reduces GI upset
notes: Hyperkalemia risk with ACEi/ARBs/K+ supplements. Active metabolite canrenone.

name: furosemide
mechanism: Inhibits Na+/K+/2Cl- cotransporter in thick ascending loop of Henle
food_effect: Food delays absorption; AUC reduced ~30%. Take on empty stomach for acute diuresis.
notes: High-dose ototoxicity risk especially with aminoglycosides. Monitor K+ — hypokalemia common.

name: hydrochlorothiazide
mechanism: Inhibits NaCl cotransporter in distal convoluted tubule
food_effect: Take in morning with food to avoid nocturia
notes: Hypokalemia, hyperuricemia, hyperglycemia risks. Less effective if CrCl <30.

name: atorvastatin
mechanism: Competitive inhibition of HMG-CoA reductase → reduces cholesterol synthesis
monitoring: LFTs at baseline, 12 weeks, then annually|CK if myopathy symptoms|Lipid panel q3-12mo
food_effect: Grapefruit juice increases AUC 2-fold. Avoid large amounts (>1 quart/day).
notes: Can be taken any time of day. Rhabdomyolysis risk with CYP3A4 inhibitors.

name: simvastatin
mechanism: Competitive inhibition of HMG-CoA reductase
food_effect: Take in EVENING — cholesterol synthesis is highest at night. Grapefruit contraindicated.
notes: 80mg dose associated with high myopathy risk — FDA limits use. Strong CYP3A4 inhibitors → rhabdomyolysis.

name: rosuvastatin
mechanism: Competitive inhibition of HMG-CoA reductase
food_effect: Can be taken any time of day, with or without food
notes: Less CYP3A4 interaction than atorvastatin/simvastatin. Asian patients may need lower doses.

name: pravastatin
mechanism: Competitive inhibition of HMG-CoA reductase
food_effect: Food reduces Cmax but not AUC. Take at bedtime.
notes: Lowest CYP interaction of all statins. Preferred in drug interaction-prone patients.

name: metformin
mechanism: Activates AMPK → reduces hepatic gluconeogenesis, improves insulin sensitivity
monitoring: HbA1c q3mo initially, then q6-12mo|Renal function annually (CrCl)|B12 q1-2yr
food_effect: Take WITH food (breakfast, dinner) — reduces GI side effects. Food reduces Cmax 25% but improves tolerability.
notes: Hold for IV contrast, surgery. Lactic acidosis risk if CrCl<30. Reduces B12 absorption.

name: glipizide
mechanism: Closes K-ATP channels in pancreatic beta cells → insulin release
food_effect: Take 30 minutes BEFORE meals. Food delays and reduces absorption significantly.
notes: Hypoglycemia risk especially in elderly/renal impairment. CYP2C9 interactions with fluconazole.

name: sitagliptin
mechanism: Inhibits DPP-4 → increases GLP-1 and GIP → glucose-dependent insulin release
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: Reduce dose in renal impairment: 50mg if CrCl 30-50; 25mg if CrCl<30.

name: empagliflozin
mechanism: Inhibits SGLT-2 in proximal tubule → glucose excretion in urine
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food, in the morning
notes: Cardiovascular and renal protection independent of glucose lowering. Hold before major surgery.

name: insulin_glargine
mechanism: Binds insulin receptor → facilitates glucose uptake into cells; suppresses gluconeogenesis/lipolysis
monitoring: Blood glucose (fasting daily)|HbA1c q3mo initially|Hypoglycemia signs|Injection site lipohypertrophy
food_effect: Subcutaneous injection — no food interaction. Consistent carbohydrate intake important for dose stability.
notes: Do not mix with other insulins. Rotate injection sites. Hypoglycemia risk increases with alcohol, beta-blockers (mask signs), ACE inhibitors. Brand-to-brand switch requires dose retitration.

name: levothyroxine
mechanism: Replaces endogenous T4, converted to active T3 in peripheral tissues
monitoring: TSH q6-8 weeks after dose change, then annually|HR|BP
food_effect: CRITICAL: Take 30-60 min BEFORE breakfast on EMPTY STOMACH. Calcium, iron, coffee, soy all reduce absorption by 20-40%. Separate 4+ hours from calcium/iron supplements.
notes: Interacts with calcium, iron, antacids (chelation), cholestyramine, sucralfate. Consistent brand important.

name: amoxicillin
mechanism: Inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis by binding PBPs
monitoring: Signs of allergic reaction|Renal function|C. diff if prolonged
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food. Food delays absorption but not extent.
notes: Penicillin allergy cross-reactivity 1-10%. Time-dependent bactericidal activity.

name: ciprofloxacin
mechanism: Inhibits DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV → DNA strand breaks
monitoring: Tendon pain (Achilles)|QT interval if cardiac risk|Blood glucose in diabetics
food_effect: Dairy products (Ca++, Mg++) and antacids chelate and reduce absorption 50-90%. Take 2 hours before or 6 hours after calcium-containing products.
notes: Strong CYP1A2 inhibitor — warfarin, theophylline, clozapine levels increase. Tendinopathy risk.

name: azithromycin
mechanism: Binds 50S ribosomal subunit → inhibits protein synthesis
monitoring: QT interval|LFTs if prolonged|Cardiac monitoring in at-risk patients
food_effect: Tablets: take with or without food. Suspension: 1hr before or 2hr after meals.
notes: QT prolongation risk — avoid with other QT-prolonging agents. Long tissue half-life.

name: clarithromycin
mechanism: Binds 50S ribosomal subunit → inhibits protein synthesis
food_effect: Take with food — reduces GI upset
notes: STRONG CYP3A4 mechanism-based inhibitor — effect persists days after stopping. Increases simvastatin/atorvastatin → rhabdomyolysis. Digoxin levels increase.

name: doxycycline
mechanism: Binds 30S ribosomal subunit → inhibits protein synthesis
food_effect: Take WITH food/milk to reduce esophageal irritation. Dairy, antacids, iron chelate — separate 2+ hours.
notes: Photosensitivity — use sunscreen. Avoid in children <8 (dental staining). GERD-like symptoms common.

name: metronidazole
mechanism: Reduced to toxic radical by anaerobic organisms → DNA strand breaks
food_effect: Take WITH food to reduce nausea
notes: DISULFIRAM-LIKE reaction with alcohol — absolute prohibition during and 48hr after course. Inhibits CYP2C9 → warfarin toxicity.

name: fluconazole
mechanism: Inhibits fungal 14-α-demethylase (CYP51) → ergosterol depletion
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: STRONG CYP2C9 inhibitor (warfarin dose reduce 50%), CYP3A4 inhibitor. QT prolongation at high doses.

name: sertraline
mechanism: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor
monitoring: Suicidal ideation (first 4 weeks)|Serotonin syndrome signs|Hyponatremia in elderly
food_effect: Take with food to improve bioavailability and reduce nausea. Bioavailability increases 30% with food.
notes: Serotonin syndrome risk with MAOIs, tramadol, triptans. Discontinuation syndrome if stopped abruptly.

name: fluoxetine
mechanism: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: Norfluoxetine active metabolite t1/2 = 4-16 days. Significant CYP2D6 inhibitor. Long washout needed before MAOI.

name: escitalopram
mechanism: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor — S-enantiomer of citalopram
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: QT prolongation at high doses. Max 10mg in hepatic impairment or elderly.

name: alprazolam
mechanism: Positive allosteric modulator of GABA-A receptors → CNS depression
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food. Grapefruit increases levels significantly.
notes: High dependence risk. Taper slowly — withdrawal can be life-threatening. Avoid alcohol.

name: phenytoin
mechanism: Blocks voltage-gated sodium channels in neuronal membranes
monitoring: Phenytoin level 2 weeks post-change|CBC annually|Gingival hyperplasia|Nystagmus
food_effect: Enteral feeds reduce absorption — hold feeds 2hr before/after. Consistent food intake important.
notes: Non-linear (saturable) kinetics — small dose changes → large level changes. Induces CYP3A4/2C9.

name: levetiracetam
mechanism: Binds SV2A synaptic vesicle protein → modulates neurotransmitter release
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food. Food reduces Cmax slightly but not AUC.
notes: Minimal drug interactions (no CYP metabolism). Dose reduce in renal impairment. Irritability common.

name: salbutamol
mechanism: Beta-2 adrenergic agonist → bronchial smooth muscle relaxation
food_effect: Inhaled: no food effect. Oral: food delays absorption.
notes: PRN use for acute bronchospasm. Frequent use (>2x/week) indicates poor control — reassess therapy.

name: montelukast
mechanism: Blocks CysLT1 receptors → reduces bronchoconstriction and inflammation
monitoring: Neuropsychiatric events (mood changes, suicidality — FDA warning)
food_effect: Take in EVENING (allergic rhinitis/asthma). Standard tablet: no food effect. Chewable: avoid for 1hr after.
notes: FDA boxed warning for neuropsychiatric events. Reserve for when ICS preferred but not tolerated.

name: omeprazole
mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits H+/K+-ATPase (proton pump) in gastric parietal cells
food_effect: Take 30-60 min BEFORE eating (breakfast preferred). Acid needed to activate. Food reduces absorption if taken simultaneously.
notes: Irreversible — effect lasts days despite short half-life. CYP2C19 PM have 3-5x exposure. Reduces clopidogrel activation.

name: pantoprazole
mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits H+/K+-ATPase
food_effect: Take 30-60 min before meal. Food does not significantly affect AUC.
notes: Fewer CYP interactions than omeprazole — preferred if on clopidogrel.

name: ondansetron
mechanism: Blocks serotonin 5-HT3 receptors centrally and peripherally → antiemesis
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: QT prolongation — avoid in hepatic impairment >8mg IV. Serotonin syndrome risk with SSRIs (rare).

name: ibuprofen
mechanism: Non-selective COX-1/COX-2 inhibitor → reduced prostaglandin synthesis
food_effect: Take WITH food or milk — reduces GI irritation. Does not affect absorption.
notes: Renal prostaglandin inhibition → AKI risk. Avoid with ACEi/ARBs/diuretics. Antihypertensive interference.

name: naproxen
mechanism: Non-selective COX inhibitor
food_effect: Take WITH food or milk
notes: Longer half-life than ibuprofen → twice daily dosing. Same GI/renal risks as all NSAIDs.

name: celecoxib
mechanism: Selective COX-2 inhibition → reduced prostaglandin synthesis with less COX-1 effect
food_effect: Food increases absorption ~20%. Can be taken with or without food.
notes: Less GI bleeding vs non-selective NSAIDs. Still renal and cardiovascular risk. CYP2C9 substrate.

name: allopurinol
mechanism: Inhibits xanthine oxidase → reduced uric acid synthesis
food_effect: Take WITH food to reduce GI upset. Take AFTER meals.
notes: Azathioprine/6-MP interaction — xanthine oxidase inhibition → mercaptopurine toxicity (reduce dose 75%). HLA-B*5801 screening in Asian patients.

name: alendronate
mechanism: Inhibits osteoclast-mediated bone resorption
monitoring: DEXA q2yr|Ca++, Vitamin D levels|Jaw pain (osteonecrosis)|Atypical femur fractures
food_effect: CRITICAL: Take with FULL GLASS of water (240mL), EMPTY STOMACH, 30 min before any food/drink/medication. REMAIN UPRIGHT 30 min after. Any food reduces absorption >90%.
notes: Esophageal irritation/ulceration if not taken correctly. Weekly dosing preferred for adherence.

name: calcium_carbonate
mechanism: Provides elemental calcium for bone mineralization; antacid
food_effect: Take WITH food — requires gastric acid for absorption. Calcium citrate can be taken without food.
notes: Chelates levothyroxine, fluoroquinolones, iron, bisphosphonates — separate 2-4 hours. Constipation common.

name: theophylline
mechanism: Inhibits phosphodiesterase → increased cAMP; adenosine antagonism
food_effect: High-fat meals may increase AUC for sustained-release formulations. Coffee/caffeine additive toxicity.
notes: CYP1A2 inducers (smoking, rifampicin) dramatically reduce levels. Fluoroquinolones increase levels. Narrow TI.

name: prednisolone
mechanism: Binds glucocorticoid receptors → anti-inflammatory gene expression changes
therapeutic_range: Clinical response; minimize to lowest effective dose
food_effect: Take WITH food to reduce GI irritation
notes: HPA axis suppression with prolonged use — taper slowly. Adrenal crisis risk if stopped abruptly.

name: azathioprine
mechanism: Prodrug → 6-MP → inhibits purine synthesis → lymphocyte proliferation suppression
monitoring: CBC weekly for 8 weeks, then monthly|LFTs|TPMT genotype before starting
food_effect: Take WITH food to reduce nausea
notes: CRITICAL: Allopurinol inhibits xanthine oxidase → 6-MP accumulation → fatal myelosuppression. TPMT testing essential.

name: cyclosporine
mechanism: Binds cyclophilin → inhibits calcineurin → reduced IL-2 transcription → T-cell suppression
food_effect: Consistent food intake essential — high-fat meal increases AUC. Grapefruit increases levels significantly.
notes: Extreme drug interaction profile (CYP3A4/P-gp). Nephrotoxic. Hypertension, hyperlipidemia common.

name: hydroxychloroquine
mechanism: Alkalinizes lysosomes → inhibits antigen processing; anti-inflammatory
food_effect: Take WITH food or milk — reduces GI effects
notes: Retinopathy risk with cumulative dose >5 mg/kg/day. QT prolongation. Safe in pregnancy for lupus.

name: morphine
mechanism: Binds mu-opioid receptors in CNS and periphery → analgesia, sedation, euphoria
food_effect: Extended-release: do not crush or chew. Food increases Cmax for IR formulation slightly.
notes: Active metabolite morphine-6-glucuronide (M6G) accumulates in renal failure — reduce dose or increase interval. Naloxone reversal available.

name: codeine
mechanism: Prodrug → O-demethylation by CYP2D6 → morphine (active) → mu-opioid receptor agonism
food_effect: Can be taken with food to reduce GI upset
notes: CYP2D6 ultra-rapid metabolizers (URM): life-threatening morphine toxicity. CONTRAINDICATED in children post-tonsillectomy and breastfeeding. PM: no analgesic effect.

name: tramadol
mechanism: Weak mu-opioid agonism + serotonin/norepinephrine reuptake inhibition
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: Serotonin syndrome risk with SSRIs/SNRIs/MAOIs. Lowers seizure threshold. CYP2D6 inhibitors reduce analgesic efficacy.

name: fentanyl
mechanism: Potent mu-opioid receptor agonist; high lipophilicity → rapid CNS entry
food_effect: Transdermal: heat (fever, electric blanket) increases absorption 3x — avoid. Sublingual: do not eat/drink for 15 min.
contraindications: acute or severe bronchial asthma|opioid non-tolerant patients (high-strength patches)
notes: 100x more potent than morphine. Transdermal takes 12-24hr to reach steady state. CYP3A4 inhibitors dramatically increase levels.

name: oxycodone
mechanism: Mu-opioid receptor agonist
monitoring: Respiratory rate|Abuse potential assessment|Hepatic function (combo with APAP)
food_effect: High-fat meal increases Cmax 25% for ER formulation. IR: take with or without food.
notes: ER formulation: do not crush/chew (dose dumping risk). Combination products contain paracetamol — monitor total APAP dose.

name: haloperidol
mechanism: D2 receptor antagonism in mesolimbic pathway
monitoring: EPS (AIMS scale)|QTc interval|Metabolic panel|Prolactin if symptomatic
food_effect: Take with food to reduce GI effects. Oral solution: dilute in water or juice.
notes: Highest EPS risk of all antipsychotics. QT prolongation. Avoid IV route due to arrhythmia risk. Depot (decanoate) available for adherence.

name: olanzapine
mechanism: D2, 5-HT2A, H1, M1, alpha-1 antagonism
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: High metabolic side effect profile (weight gain, T2DM, dyslipidaemia). Smoking induces CYP1A2 — smokers need higher dose; dose reduction on smoking cessation.

name: quetiapine
mechanism: D2, 5-HT2A, H1, alpha-1 antagonism; lower D2 affinity than haloperidol
food_effect: Food increases Cmax 25-52% — take consistently (always with or always without food).
notes: Low EPS risk. QT prolongation. Strong sedation (H1). CYP3A4 inducers (carbamazepine) dramatically reduce levels — may need 5x dose increase.

name: risperidone
mechanism: D2 and 5-HT2A antagonism; also alpha-1, H1
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: CYP2D6 PMs: higher risperidone, less 9-OH-risperidone; similar clinical effect but adjust for tolerability. Depot (Risperdal Consta) q2wk.

name: clozapine
mechanism: D4 > D2 antagonism; 5-HT2, H1, M1, alpha antagonism; superior efficacy in treatment-resistant schizophrenia
monitoring: ANC weekly for 6 months, then bi-weekly, then monthly (agranulocytosis)|Weight|Glucose|Lipids|BP|ECG
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
contraindications: history of clozapine-induced agranulocytosis|uncontrolled epilepsy|severe CNS depression
notes: REMS program required. Agranulocytosis risk — absolute neutrophil count monitoring mandatory. Myocarditis risk in first 4 weeks. Smoking cessation dramatically increases levels (CYP1A2).

name: acyclovir
mechanism: Thymidine kinase phosphorylation → acyclovir triphosphate inhibits HSV/VZV DNA polymerase
monitoring: Renal function (IV — crystals can precipitate)|Hydration|CBC in immunosuppressed
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food. Adequate hydration required (especially IV — prevents crystalluria).
notes: IV: hydrate well to prevent tubular crystallization. Dose reduce if CrCl <50. Valacyclovir is oral prodrug with 3-5x better bioavailability.

name: valacyclovir
mechanism: Prodrug of acyclovir; L-valine ester → better oral bioavailability → same mechanism as acyclovir
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: Preferred oral formulation over acyclovir for bioavailability. Dose reduce if CrCl <50.

name: oseltamivir
mechanism: Inhibits influenza neuraminidase → prevents viral release from infected cells
therapeutic_range: Initiated within 48hr of symptom onset for efficacy
monitoring: Renal function (dose adjust if CrCl <30)|Neuropsychiatric symptoms (children)
food_effect: Take with food to reduce nausea/vomiting
notes: Must start within 48hr of onset. Dose reduce for CrCl 10-30: 75mg once daily. Suspicion in pediatrics for neuropsychiatric events.

name: tenofovir
mechanism: Nucleotide reverse transcriptase inhibitor → competitive inhibition of HIV-1 RT and HBV polymerase
monitoring: Renal function (tubular toxicity)|Bone mineral density|Phosphate|HIV VL|HBV DNA
food_effect: High-fat meal increases AUC 40% — take consistently with or without food.
notes: Fanconi syndrome risk (proximal tubular dysfunction). BMD loss — calcium/Vit D supplementation. TDF replaced by TAF (lower renal/bone toxicity) in many settings.

name: emtricitabine
mechanism: Cytidine analogue NRTI → inhibits HIV-1 reverse transcriptase and HBV polymerase
monitoring: Renal function|HIV VL|HBV status before discontinuation (flare risk)
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: Usually co-formulated with TDF as Truvada or with TAF as Descovy. HBV flare if stopped in HBV co-infected — do not discontinue without HBV plan.

name: carbamazepine
mechanism: Inhibits voltage-gated Na+ channels → stabilizes neuronal membranes; also reduces glutamate release
monitoring: Carbamazepine level (at steady state 2-4wk)|CBC (aplastic anemia)|LFTs|Na+ (SIADH)|HLA-B*1502 screening (Asian patients — SJS risk)
food_effect: Take WITH food to reduce GI upset. Grapefruit increases levels significantly.
contraindications: bone marrow suppression|concurrent MAOIs|HLA-B*1502 positive (SJS risk in Asians)
notes: POTENT CYP3A4, CYP2C9, CYP1A2 INDUCER — reduces levels of oral contraceptives, warfarin, statins, many others. Autoinduction (induces own metabolism — t1/2 shortens from 65hr initially to 25hr at steady state). SJS/TEN risk — HLA-B*1502 test in at-risk populations.

name: valproate
mechanism: Na+ channel inhibition, GABA transaminase inhibition (↑ GABA), histone deacetylase inhibition
food_effect: Take WITH food to reduce GI upset. Enteric-coated formulation reduces GI side effects.
contraindications: hepatic disease|urea cycle disorders|pregnancy (teratogen — neural tube defects, cognitive impairment)
notes: TERATOGEN — neural tube defects (2-3%), cognitive impairment in children. Contraindicated in women of childbearing potential unless no alternative. Inhibits CYP2C9 → increases lamotrigine, phenobarbital. Carbapenems dramatically reduce valproate levels.

name: lamotrigine
mechanism: Inhibits voltage-gated Na+ channels; reduces glutamate/aspartate release
monitoring: Lamotrigine level|Skin rash (SJS/TEN — especially if dose escalated too fast)|Seizure frequency
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: SJS risk — SLOW titration mandatory (start 25mg, double q2wk). Valproate doubles lamotrigine level — reduce dose 50%. Carbamazepine/phenytoin halve lamotrigine level. OCP reduces lamotrigine level 50% — adjust on initiation/cessation.

name: lithium
mechanism: Inositol monophosphatase inhibition → affects intracellular signaling; modulates GSK-3β, serotonin/dopamine release
monitoring: Lithium level (12hr post-dose) q3-6mo|Renal function (GFR — lithium nephrotoxicity)|Thyroid (hypothyroidism)|Ca++ (hyperparathyroidism)|ECG (T-wave changes)
food_effect: Take with food/milk to reduce GI upset. MAINTAIN CONSISTENT SODIUM INTAKE — low-sodium diet causes lithium retention → toxicity. Dehydration causes toxicity.
notes: CRITICAL: NSAIDs, thiazides, ACE inhibitors reduce renal lithium clearance → toxicity. Dehydration/sodium restriction → lithium toxicity. Toxicity signs: tremor, ataxia, confusion, arrhythmia. No protein binding → dialysis removes lithium in overdose.

name: vancomycin
mechanism: Inhibits cell wall synthesis by binding D-Ala-D-Ala terminus of peptidoglycan precursors
monitoring: AUC-guided TDM (preferred) or trough|Renal function (nephrotoxicity)|Audiology if prolonged (ototoxicity)|Red Man syndrome (infusion rate)
food_effect: IV only (systemic). Oral formulation for C. diff — not absorbed systemically.
notes: Red Man Syndrome: infuse over ≥60 min. Nephrotoxicity synergistic with aminoglycosides. Oral vancomycin for C. diff: NOT absorbed — safe in renal impairment. MRSA drug of choice.

name: meropenem
mechanism: Inhibits PBPs → blocks cell wall transpeptidation → bactericidal
monitoring: Renal function (dose adjust)|Seizure threshold (especially with prior CNS disease)|Valproate level (carbapenems reduce valproate)
food_effect: IV only
notes: CRITICAL: Carbapenems reduce valproate serum level up to 90% within 24hr — do not combine if valproate essential. Extended infusion (3hr) recommended for MIC >2 mg/L organisms.

name: linezolid
mechanism: Binds 23S rRNA of 50S ribosomal subunit → inhibits initiation complex formation
monitoring: CBC weekly (myelosuppression)|Serotonin syndrome signs|Lactic acidosis (mitochondrial toxicity, prolonged use)|Optic neuropathy (prolonged use >28 days)
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food. AVOID tyramine-rich foods (aged cheese, cured meats, wine) — MAOI-like reaction.
notes: Weak MAO inhibitor — serotonin syndrome risk with SSRIs/SNRIs/tramadol (critical DDI). Tyramine dietary restriction required. Not intended for >28-day courses without specialist review.

name: insulin_aspart
mechanism: Rapid-onset insulin receptor agonism — covers post-meal glucose excursion
monitoring: Blood glucose before and 2hr after meals|Hypoglycemia|HbA1c
food_effect: MUST be administered 0-15 minutes before or immediately after starting a meal. Dose based on carbohydrate content.
notes: Onset 10-20 min; peak 1-3hr; duration 3-5hr. High hypoglycemia risk if meal skipped after injection.

name: contraceptive_pill_ethinyl_estradiol
mechanism: Estrogen + progestin → suppresses LH/FSH surges → inhibits ovulation; thickens cervical mucus; alters endometrium
monitoring: BP annually|Breast exam|Cervical smear|VTE risk assessment|Migraine pattern
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food. Take at same time each day.
contraindications: migraine with aura|age >35 + smoking|VTE history|estrogen-sensitive cancer|severe hypertension
notes: ENZYME INDUCERS (rifampicin, carbamazepine, phenytoin, St. John's Wort) reduce efficacy — use barrier method + higher-dose OCP. Lamotrigine clearance increased significantly by OCP — monitor seizure control.

name: testosterone
mechanism: Binds androgen receptor → promotes male secondary sexual characteristics, spermatogenesis, erythropoiesis, muscle/bone anabolism
food_effect: Transdermal: do not apply to scrotum/damaged skin. Wash hands after. Do not shower/swim 5hr post-application.
notes: Erythrocytosis risk — hold if Hct >54%. Stimulates PSA — monitor prostate. Increases warfarin effect. Contraindicated in prostate/breast cancer.

name: levodopa_carbidopa
mechanism: Levodopa crosses BBB → DOPA decarboxylase → dopamine. Carbidopa inhibits peripheral DOPA decarboxylase → reduces levodopa side effects, increases CNS delivery
monitoring: Motor function (UPDRS)|Orthostatic BP|Dyskinesia onset|Impulse control disorders|Nausea
food_effect: HIGH-PROTEIN MEALS compete for absorption via large neutral amino acid transporters — take 30-60 min before or 1-2hr after protein-rich meals for maximal effect. Food reduces Cmax but protein is the key interaction.
notes: MAOIs (non-selective) absolutely contraindicated — hypertensive crisis. Dopamine agonists (pramipexole) added to extend honeymoon period. Wearing-off and on-off phenomena develop over time.

name: pramipexole
mechanism: Direct D2/D3 receptor agonism in striatum and substantia nigra
monitoring: Impulse control disorders (gambling, hypersexuality)|Sleep attacks/somnolence (no driving)|Orthostatic BP|Edema|Hallucinations in elderly
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: Impulse control disorders in up to 17% (gambling, shopping, hypersexuality) — counsel patients and carers. Sleep attacks without warning — no driving. Renal dose adjustment if CrCl <60.

name: baclofen
mechanism: GABA-B receptor agonism → inhibits mono- and polysynaptic spinal reflexes → muscle relaxation
food_effect: Take WITH food to reduce GI upset
notes: NEVER stop abruptly — withdrawal can cause seizures, hyperthermia, hallucinations (especially intrathecal baclofen). Taper slowly.

name: tamsulosin
mechanism: Selective alpha-1A blockade in prostate smooth muscle → relaxes bladder neck and prostate → improved urine flow
monitoring: BP (orthostasis)|Intraoperative Floppy Iris Syndrome (IFIS) — warn ophthalmologist before cataract surgery
food_effect: Take 30 min after same meal each day — food reduces peak concentration and orthostatic hypotension risk.
notes: Intraoperative Floppy Iris Syndrome — MUST inform surgeon before any eye surgery. Orthostatic hypotension worse with first dose. CYP3A4/2D6 inhibitors increase levels.

name: finasteride
mechanism: Inhibits 5-alpha reductase type II → reduces DHT → prostate size reduction / hair follicle androgen effect reduction
monitoring: PSA (finasteride reduces PSA by 50% — adjust reference range)|Sexual dysfunction|Breast tenderness
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: TERATOGENIC — pregnant women must NOT handle crushed/broken tablets. PSA is halved — a PSA that appears 'normal' may be elevated when corrected. High-grade prostate cancer concern (clinical debate).

name: acetazolamide
mechanism: Inhibits carbonic anhydrase → reduces aqueous humor formation (IOP reduction); also reduces CSF production; metabolic acidosis
monitoring: Electrolytes (K+ loss)|Renal function|Stevens-Johnson syndrome (rare)|Kidney stones (bicarbonate loss → urine alkalosis)
food_effect: Take WITH food to reduce GI upset. High potassium foods (bananas, oranges) recommended.
notes: Sulfonamide-based — cross-reactivity with sulfa allergy possible. Hypokalemia risk. Contraindicated in hepatic cirrhosis (NH3 retention). Altitude sickness prevention/treatment.

name: enoxaparin
mechanism: Activates antithrombin III with higher anti-Xa than anti-IIa activity → inhibits factor Xa predominantly
therapeutic_range: Anti-Xa 0.5-1.0 IU/mL (twice daily); 1.0-2.0 IU/mL (once daily, 4hr post-dose)
monitoring: Anti-Xa level in renal impairment/extremes of weight/pregnancy|Platelet count (HIT less common than UFH)|Renal function
food_effect: Subcutaneous injection — no food interaction
notes: Does NOT require routine monitoring in standard-weight patients with normal renal function. Anti-Xa monitoring recommended if CrCl <30, weight <45kg or >100kg, or pregnancy. Partially reversed by protamine (not fully).

name: clopidogrel
mechanism: Prodrug → CYP2C19 activation → active thiol metabolite irreversibly inhibits P2Y12 ADP receptor on platelets → reduced platelet aggregation
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: CYP2C19 *2 and *3 poor metabolizers: ~30% of Asians, 2-3% of Caucasians — reduced antiplatelet effect → consider alternative (ticagrelor, prasugrel). PPIs (especially omeprazole) inhibit CYP2C19 → reduced clopidogrel activation (controversial — pantoprazole safer).

name: aspirin
mechanism: Irreversibly acetylates COX-1 and COX-2 → reduced TXA2 (antiplatelet) and PGE2 (analgesic/anti-inflammatory)
monitoring: GI symptoms (ulcer risk)|Renal function|Salicylate level if toxicity suspected|INR if on warfarin
food_effect: Take WITH food or milk to reduce GI irritation. Enteric-coated formulation reduces direct gastric contact.
notes: Reye's syndrome in children with viral illness — avoid under 16yr. Antiplatelet effect lasts platelet lifetime (7-10 days). Tinnitus is early sign of salicylate toxicity. Interacts with warfarin (bleeding), methotrexate (toxicity), ACE inhibitors (reduced effect).

name: methotrexate
mechanism: Inhibits dihydrofolate reductase (DHFR) → reduces folate cofactors → inhibits DNA/RNA synthesis and purine metabolism; anti-inflammatory at low doses via adenosine pathway
therapeutic_range: Rheumatology: clinical response; Oncology: dose-dependent with leucovorin rescue
monitoring: LFTs monthly (hepatotoxicity → fibrosis)|CBC monthly (myelosuppression)|Renal function|Pulmonary function (pneumonitis)|Folate supplementation
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food. Avoid alcohol (hepatotoxicity).
notes: TERATOGEN — strict contraception required during and 6 months after. Take folic acid 5mg weekly (NOT on MTX day) to reduce side effects. NSAIDs + MTX = toxicity risk (reduce renal clearance). Trimethoprim + MTX = severe myelosuppression (additive DHFR inhibition). Weekly dosing for rheumatology — NEVER daily.

name: budesonide
mechanism: Binds glucocorticoid receptors → reduces airway inflammation, edema, mucus secretion
monitoring: Height in children (growth velocity)|Adrenal suppression (high doses)|Bone density (long-term)|Oropharyngeal candidiasis
food_effect: Inhaled: rinse mouth after use to reduce oropharyngeal candidiasis. Oral capsule (Crohn's): swallow whole, do not crush.
notes: Minimal systemic absorption vs oral steroids. CYP3A4 inhibitors (itraconazole, ritonavir) can increase systemic budesonide → adrenal suppression. Rinse mouth after inhaled use.

name: ipratropium
mechanism: Competitively inhibits M1/M3 muscarinic receptors in bronchial smooth muscle → bronchodilation; reduces secretions
monitoring: Urinary retention (BPH)|Angle-closure glaucoma if eye contact with solution
food_effect: Inhaled — no food interaction
notes: Avoid contact with eyes (nebulizer mask) — angle-closure glaucoma risk. Additive anticholinergic effects with other antimuscarinics. Less effective for acute asthma than SABA.

name: donepezil
mechanism: Reversible inhibition of acetylcholinesterase → increases acetylcholine in cholinergic synapses of basal forebrain → improves cognition in Alzheimer's
monitoring: GI side effects (nausea, diarrhea)|Bradycardia/syncope|Sleep disturbances (nightmares — take in AM)
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food. If nightmares occur, switch to morning dosing.
notes: Bradycardia/syncope — caution with sick sinus syndrome, AV block. NSAIDs risk GI bleeding with cholinesterase inhibitors. Higher dose (23mg) more GI side effects.

name: diazepam
mechanism: Positive allosteric modulator at GABA-A receptors → enhances Cl- influx → CNS depression
therapeutic_range: Clinical anxiolysis/seizure control; avoid respiratory depression
food_effect: Take with food to reduce GI effects. Alcohol: ABSOLUTE AVOIDANCE — additive CNS/respiratory depression.
notes: Active metabolite desmethyldiazepam t1/2 = 36-200hr — accumulates in elderly. Withdrawal (after >2-4 weeks): taper slowly to prevent seizures. Flumazenil reversal. Avoid in elderly (Beers criteria).

name: zolpidem
mechanism: Selective GABA-A alpha-1 subunit PAM → hypnotic with less anxiolysis/muscle relaxation than BZDs
monitoring: Next-day sedation|Parasomnias (sleep-driving, sleep-eating)|Dependence potential|CYP3A4 inhibitors (double levels)
food_effect: Take on EMPTY STOMACH — food delays onset by 1hr and reduces Cmax. Take immediately before bed.
notes: Reduce dose to 5mg in women (lower clearance), elderly, hepatic impairment. Parasomnias (complex sleep behaviors) — warn patients. Not for long-term use. CYP3A4 inhibitors (ketoconazole) double levels.

name: venlafaxine
mechanism: Serotonin and norepinephrine reuptake inhibition (NE selectivity increases at higher doses)
monitoring: BP (dose-dependent hypertension — check q3mo)|Discontinuation syndrome (taper slowly)|Serotonin syndrome signs
food_effect: Take with food to reduce nausea. Extended-release: swallow whole.
notes: Dose-dependent hypertension (especially >300mg) — monitor BP. Severe discontinuation syndrome — taper over weeks. Serotonin syndrome with MAOIs/tramadol. CYP2D6 inhibitors increase levels.

name: bupropion
mechanism: Inhibits reuptake of norepinephrine and dopamine; no serotonergic activity
monitoring: Seizure threshold (dose-related — do not exceed 150mg per dose)|BP|Insomnia|Suicidal ideation initially
food_effect: Take with food to reduce nausea. Extended-release: do not crush.
notes: Lowers seizure threshold — CONTRAINDICATED in eating disorders (electrolyte imbalances), seizure history, concurrent CNS stimulants. No sexual dysfunction (unlike SSRIs). Strong CYP2D6 inhibitor — increases tamoxifen, tricyclics, many others.

name: sulfasalazine
mechanism: Cleaved by colonic bacteria → mesalazine (anti-inflammatory) + sulfapyridine (immunomodulatory, DMARD component)
food_effect: Take WITH food to reduce GI side effects. Ensure adequate hydration.
notes: G6PD deficiency → hemolytic anemia. NAT2 slow acetylators (50% of Caucasians) higher sulfapyridine levels → more side effects. Reduces folate absorption — supplement. Orange urine/contact lens staining (warn patients).

name: tamoxifen
mechanism: Competitive estrogen receptor antagonism in breast tissue; partial agonism in uterus/bone
therapeutic_range: Clinical (endoxifen level >5.97 ng/mL associated with benefit)
monitoring: Annual gynecology (endometrial cancer risk)|DVT/PE symptoms|Ophthalmology if visual symptoms|CYP2D6 genotype|Endoxifen level optional
food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
notes: CYP2D6 METABOLIZES to endoxifen (active metabolite — main efficacy driver). CYP2D6 inhibitors (fluoxetine, paroxetine, bupropion) DRASTICALLY reduce endoxifen → reduced efficacy. Avoid CYP2D6 inhibitors during tamoxifen therapy. Uterine cancer risk — any abnormal bleeding requires urgent investigation. VTE risk — thromboprophylaxis if major surgery.

name: imatinib
mechanism: Competitive inhibition of ATP binding site of BCR-ABL kinase → blocks CML proliferation; also inhibits c-KIT (GIST) and PDGFR
monitoring: CBC (cytopenias)|LFTs|Fluid retention/edema (pericardial, pleural)|Bone marrow response (BCR-ABL PCR)|CYP3A4 interactions
food_effect: Take WITH food and large glass of water to reduce GI irritation
notes: CYP3A4 inducers (rifampicin, carbamazepine) dramatically reduce levels — avoid or increase dose. CYP3A4 inhibitors (ketoconazole) increase levels. Fluid retention common — weigh patients regularly.
